title: RFC 9309 Robots Semantics and Library Conformance Adapter · TypeScript Build Room
summary: A disability-led public-interest technology team incrementally builds RFC 9309 decision adapter around robots-parser from original deterministic HTTP, robots, sitemap, HTML, browser, and report fixtures while retaining prior TypeScript, Node, web, Git, accessibility, security, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of RFC 9309 decision adapter around robots-parser while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, e…
objectives: Construct the RFC 9309 retrieval URL from the target service origin and send one stable truthful crawler product token with an accountable contact.
Apply group selection, percent encoding, longest-match precedence, and allow-over-disallow tie behavior from RFC 9309.
Classify successful, redirecting, unavailable, unreachable, oversized, malformed, and stale robots responses conservatively.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Robots Location Agent
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 6687 during an isolated browser, package, and restore smoke. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove RFC 9309 product, group, encoding, longest-match, response-state, cache, and library adapter behavior; every Promise, stream, listener, timer, socket, file, browser context, and report sink needs one cleanup owner; then change one test, mutation, package, browser binary, container, capacity, incident, restore, rollback, or residual-risk condition and reconcile release evidence. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Construct the RFC 9309 retrieval URL from the target service origin and send one stable truthful crawler product token with an accountable contact.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Robots Location Agent.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Robots Location Agent, keep Robots Location Agent connected to executable TypeScript authorization, URL, runtime admission, Fetch, Promise, browser, accessibility, report, package, and recovery decision evidence.

steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: crawler-ts-extension-restraint-yc093c" that uses case 3552 to prove RFC 9309 product, group, encoding, longest-match, response-state, cache, and library adapter behavior. Keep defaults runnable and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import Node APIs, open streams or sockets, resolve DNS, parse through Cheerio, launch Playwright or axe-core, access files or packages, retain credentials, leave handles, or claim native runtime, accessibility, privacy, legal, load, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Node 24.18.0, TypeScript 7.0.2 and the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility compiler API, Cheerio 1.2.0, robots-parser 3.0.1, p-limit 7.3.0, csv-stringify 6.8.1, Playwright 1.61.1, axe-core 4.12.1, original fixtures, loopback servers, emitted-runtime and handle tests, controlled DNS and browser environments, accessibility review, packages, containers, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.
